source.visibleThreadHeading: 34 thoughts on “Pokemon Crystal Advance Redux Cheats”
source.authority: Public reader comments; lower authority than the maintained article and official hack sources

decisions.outcome: Generate bag slot 2 item choices from the 343-row tested comment table plus the later mint corrections/additions; exclude key, story and unknown entries from the main file.
decisions.reason: Three replies report the replacement address working; an older report says the result appears in bag slot 2 with quantity 99. The newest complaint checked the PC, which is consistent with the replacement being a bag modifier rather than a PC modifier.

decisions.outcome: Remove all 463 concrete wild encounter choices from the main RetroArch file.
decisions.reason: The code was reported working on the April 2 build, but a newer May report says it produces Bad Eggs and the article's closing text says Pokémon modifiers are not working.

decisions.outcome: Remove the shiny encounter entry from the main RetroArch file.
decisions.reason: Two users report failure on recent 2026 builds, including a direct newest-update retry after the site maintainer suggested the published code.

decisions.outcome: Do not add the vague Pokémon code, March 2025 starter/gift modifier or March 2025 wild modifier sequences.
decisions.reason: The suggestions are vague, explicitly unverified, reported not working, or only claimed for the March 2025 build.

decisions.outcome: Keep the later June 25 Poké Mart table, but omit badges, key/story items and unknown/unused entries from the main file.
decisions.reason: The June table was promoted into the article and described as tested; its own warning advises avoiding story/key items. The newest PC complaint does not test the Poké Mart address.

items.name: Master Ball
items.notes: The ultimate Poké Ball that catches any wild Pokémon without fail.

items.name: Luxury Ball
items.notes: Originally noted as “Friendship Ball”, official name is Luxury Ball.

items.name: Max Potion
items.notes: Original text called it “Full Restore”.

items.name: Full Heal
items.notes: Original text called it “Universal Potion”.

items.notes: Original text called it “Mental Herb”.

items.name: Max Elixir
items.notes: Original text called it “Full PP Restore”.

items.notes: Original text called it “PP raised to maximum”.

items.notes: Original text has a typo.

items.name: Leaf Stone

items.name: Ability Capsule
items.notes: Conflicts with web version’s Mail code, actual test prevails.

items.name: Heal Orb
items.notes: Restores HP at the end of the turn. Custom sprite resembles Leftovers.
